steps.title: Trace the passage's two opposing signals
steps.content:
The first paragraph establishes the old man's public displeasure: he looks at his other pictures, decides the new one doesn't belong, and fumes "Don't belong in here." Then the second paragraph pivots with "And yet the old man was secretly proud of his acquisition." That pivot—open criticism versus hidden pride—is the engine of the passage. The main purpose is to show these two feelings existing at once.
  • Choice A — To reveal the shop owner's conflicted feelings about the new picture: Matches the open fuming and the hidden pride. ✓
  • Choice B — To convey the shop owner's resentment of the person he got the new picture from: The young seller is mentioned only to explain how the owner acquired the picture; no resentment toward him is expressed. ✗
  • Choice C — To describe the items that the shop owner most highly prizes: The passage lists other pictures only to show the new one doesn't fit—it never ranks what the owner prizes most. ✗
  • Choice D — To explain differences between the new picture and other pictures in the shop: No visual or thematic details of the new picture are given, so no meaningful comparison is made. ✗

steps.title: Confirm the match
steps.content: The passage's structure—public dismissal followed by secret pride—exists to show conflicted feelings. That is the correct answer.
